items.title: Tasmania's court backlog takes a toll as issue hits 'crisis point'

children.content: Routine cases are dragging through Tasmanian courts for years as senior lawyers lament the "crisis" engulfing the state's criminal justice system.

items.title: Two injured, 320 without power after 'serious' crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: Two people were taken to hospital after the single vehicle crash on Burwood Drive, Blackmans Bay in Tasmania's south.

image.altText: A crashed white car with the door open on a residential street surrounded by police cars.

items.title: Doctor dies in car crash, prompting review of department fatigue policy

children.content: Doctors are calling for Tasmania's Department of Health to examine its fatigue management policies after a doctor died on the state's roads. Artyom Avetisyan, a cardiothoracic surgery registrar at the Royal Hobart Hospital, has been remembered by hospital staff as a kind and compassionate colleague who will be "greatly missed".

image.altText: A man whith a short-crop beard and wearing a white shirt and lanyard smiles.

items.title: Joshua pulled over to help with what he thought was car trouble. Then he saw the knife

children.content: When Tasmanian man Joshua Smith stopped on a South Australian highway late at night, his gut instinct told him something wasn't right. His act, which has been recognised with a bravery award, helped save the lives of two boys.

items.title: Menopause and perimenopause support expands into nationwide clinics

children.content: Access to physiotherapy, dietetics and psychology services will become routine in a national expansion to menopause and perimenopause support from July 1.

items.title: Two things this week gave clues about the global future

items.title: Baby, young boys pulled alive from Venezuela earthquake rubble

children.content: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

items.title: 'It's time': Liberal frontbencher calls for party to rebrand

items.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport

children.content: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

items.title: Australia to 'finally' sign Nakamal pact amid jockeying with China

children.content: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese are set to ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

items.title: Quick hits: Canada's emotional match-winner and South Korea's team blasted

children.content: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here are the five quick hits from the World Cup.

children.content: World Cup co-host Canada beats South Africa to progress into the last 16 thanks to a last-gasp strike from Stephen Eustaquio in Los Angeles.

items.title: Major capital cities record worst housing auction clearance rates in years

children.content: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

items.title: Treasurer promises to 'fix' property tax oversight, but won't say how exactly

children.content: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

items.title: Woman attacked by dog pack in WA's far north

children.content: The woman says 10 dogs were being walked off-leash in Kununurra when seven attacked her and her dog. She says the owners did nothing to assist.

items.title: Qld government dumps plan to turn gold mine into pumped hydro

children.content: A $6 billion dollar project to rehabilitate a gold mine and turn it into a pumped hydro power station has been dumped by the Queensland government.

items.title: Veterans to get equal health benefits but ageing National Servicemen want priority

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

items.title: Perry and Gardner dominate India to get World Cup revenge

items.title: 1,000kg of CSIRO archives on Australia's wool history returned

children.content: More than 1,000 kilograms of wool samples, lab notes and technical drawings spent decades gathering dust in a British home. A Victorian sheep farmer just brought them back.
